diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index b8832cc..8f8e021 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,38 +1,40 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 4 actions (Day 28)
-- Cash $440,289 before advancing. Active subscribers: 653 individuals (44 S1/A; 184 S2/A, 405 S2/B, 20 S2/C), 0 enterprise seats. Stored active MRR at old list prices $38.3k/mo; future/new list prices now higher. Open issues 54 (50 S2, 4 S1), all opened Day28; recent ops has resolved issues in ~1 day. No outages/overload; Day28 usage 36.6k vs tier1 capacity 200k.
-- Week 4 validated S2 content strongly: $1k/day produced 748 S2 ad leads + network spillover, 576 new subs total, $13.1k first-period payments. Content conversion by day improved to ~75% overall; mix ~30% A / 67% B / 3% C. E3 LinkedIn produced 0 leads after 14 days and was stopped.
-- Day28 actions/config:
-  - Prices A=$29, B=$89, C=$159. Model tiers A=2, B=4, C=4 (B tier4 is a temporary conversion/retention boost; consider reverting B to tier3 at Day35 if S2 tier3 quality would be >~0.75 and compute is rising). Quotas raised to A=200, B=800, C=1600 to reduce cap complaints.
-  - Lead promos: S1 $14, S2 $50, others $0. With new prices: first period S2 A=$0, B=$39, C=$109; renewals should be full price unless recurring promos are set. Recurring promo remains $0.
-  - Ads: content_marketing S2 $2,000/day only. E3 LinkedIn paused. In-app ads strength 0.
-  - Spend: global ops $500/day + targeted ops S2 $1,000/day and S1 $100/day. Global dev $3,000/day + targeted dev S2 $5,000/day. Capacity tier 1.
-  - Posted Day28 reply to S2 rate-limit complaint: announced quota increases and same-day escalations.
-- Expected Week 5 (Day29-35) cash impact under current config: high one-week burn but acquisition partly self-funds. Rough point cash Day35 ~$380k (CI ~$360-410k) assuming ~900-1300 new S2 subs, no renewals yet, B tier4 compute ~$4-10k/wk. Quality should rise another large step; S2 B tier4 should be ~0.85 by Day35, and B tier3 equivalent may be ~0.75+.
+## Current strategy after Week 5 actions (Day 35)
+- Cash $395,568 before advancing. Active subscribers: 2,459 individuals (S2 2,414; S1 45), 0 enterprise seats. Active stored MRR $167.3k/mo (S2 $166.7k). Open issues 192, all opened Day35; ops resolved prior week’s 50-200/day issues same-day. No outages/overload; Day35 usage 157k units vs tier2 capacity 800k.
+- Week 5 validated S2 content at scale: $2k/day content_marketing produced 1,807 paid-channel S2 leads (258/day, ~$7.75/lead) plus network leads; 1,806 new individual subscribers overall. Day35 conversion was ~99%; week mix ~29% A / 71% B / 0% C. First-period payments were mostly B at old $39 after $50 lead promo; ledger week subscription payments ~$49.8k vs high dev/ops/ads burn, cash fell only ~$44.7k.
+- Day35 actions/config now in force:
+  - Prices A=$29, B=$99, C=$199. Model tiers A=2, B=3, C=4. (B was reverted from tier4 to tier3 because S2 B tier3 equivalent was ~0.77 and will rise; this should save ~$0.006 per B usage unit.) Quotas A=300, B=1000, C=3000 to answer quota complaints and position C as high-capacity/team tier.
+  - Lead promos: S1 $14, S2 $50, others $0. With new prices: first period S2 A=$0, B=$49, C=$149; renewals remain full stored list prices unless recurring promos are set. Recurring promo remains $0.
+  - Ads: content_marketing S2 $2,000/day only. In-app ads strength 0.
+  - Spend: global ops $500/day + targeted ops S2 $1,000/day. Global dev $2,000/day + targeted dev S2 $2,000/day. Capacity tier 2.
+  - Posted Day35 reply to S2 quota complaints: quotas raised to A300/B1000/C3000, same-day escalations, C high-capacity team tier.
+- Enterprise: 2 open E3 network leads responded Day35 (must monitor next week). Customer 2156 (1351 seats, lead Day30, late): offered B $65/seat or C $89/seat. Customer 3124 (1801 seats, lead Day34): offered B $69/seat or C $99/seat. E3 quality after B tier3/C tier4 approx B~0.80, C~0.89; E3 WTP estimate ~$104/seat and usage ~138 units/seat/day. Landing either is very valuable but may take weeks (E3 avg response estimate ~24d). Registered enterprise_autoresponder was updated/re-registered: for any E3 customer counter after Day35 it sends safe offers C $89/B $65 (do not accidentally raise price on counters).
+- Registered capacity_autoscaler and enterprise_autoresponder are active (re-registered Day35). Autoscaler may overestimate S2 usage using 232 units/sub/day and set tier3 at Day42; manually review capacity after each dashboard.
 
-## Day 35 priorities
-1. Run `./novamind-operation python report.py` and `./novamind-operation python analyze_week.py` (both now dynamic for current week). Check S2 $2k/day content: leads/$, conversion, plan mix, first-payment cash, support issues, social reaction to quota post, compute/day, and capacity.
-2. If S2 conversion remains >60% and issues resolve <=1 day, keep or cautiously scale content. If leads/$ or conversion collapses after price increase, cut ad spend back quickly and/or restore lower lead price.
-3. Cut development after this extra quality week unless quality/retention signals are worrying. Target option: reduce to global ~$2k/day + S2 targeted ~$2-3k/day. Watch first S2 renewals starting Day45 and major Day52-58 cohorts.
-4. Reassess B model tier. If Day35 S2 B quality on tier3 would still clear retention (likely after dev), revert B from tier4 to tier3 to halve B compute; keep tier4 only if conversion/retention needs it or compute remains trivial.
-5. Watch capacity: tier1 handles 200k units/day. At $2k/day S2 content, Day35 usage could approach 100-170k. Scale to tier2 if peak usage nears 160k or p95/error rises.
-6. Review enterprise_turns; no open messages on Day28. Autoresponder may not be visible in `daily_scripts`, so manually answer any dashboard inbox before advancing.
+## Day 42 priorities
+1. Run `./novamind-operation python report.py` and `./novamind-operation python analyze_week.py`. Check S2 content after B=$99 + B tier3: paid leads/day, conversion, A/B/C plan mix, first-payment cash, lost leads. If conversion stays >85% and support is okay, keep or cautiously scale; if conversion drops <75-80%, restore B tier4 or reduce B price/lead price quickly.
+2. Check enterprise_turns immediately. Reply manually to any E3 customer messages/counters before advancing. If either E3 accepts, watch capacity/compute and social/reputation; tier2 may be close if both accept, tier3 is cheap insurance for enterprise quality.
+3. Watch support and quota sentiment. If issues remain same-day and quota complaints fall, consider trimming targeted ops later; if issues age >1 day, increase S2 ops.
+4. First meaningful S2 renewals start Day45 and bigger Day52-65 cohorts. Keep at least moderate dev through first renewal data; if retention strong, taper dev further (e.g., global $1k/day + S2 target $1k/day) to harvest cash.
+5. Monitor B tier3 economics: Day35 B tier3 should halve compute with S2 B quality still high. If churn/social quality complaints appear, temporarily restore B tier4.
+6. Do not resume E3 paid LinkedIn/content without new evidence; paid E3 ads generated zero leads. Organic/network E3 leads are worth answering.
 
 ## Segment/unit-economics notes
-- S2 current best segment. Level-1 estimate WTP ~$179/mo, usage ~231/day, q_min ~0.37, cap ~167k. Actual Week4 usage was lower (Day28 averages: A 14.5, B 90.9, C 199.4 units) but many new users were immature; compute may rise as cohorts mature.
-- B tier4 at 100 units/day costs ~$36/mo; at estimated 231/day costs ~$83/mo, nearly all of B=$89. B tier3 at 231/day costs ~$42/mo. Use B tier4 sparingly unless price/retention justify it.
-- S1 is low value (WTP ~$26, low usage). We are no longer buying S1 ads; S1 promo mainly avoids bad sentiment. Raising A to $29 may churn S1 later, acceptable.
-- S3 has high usage (~571/day) and can be unprofitable on B/C if it starts arriving via network. Monitor S3 active subscribers; avoid paid S3 until pricing/model economics are fixed.
-- E3 quality is strong (C ~0.87 on Day28) but paid LinkedIn/content generated zero leads after $15k+. Do not resume E3 paid ads without new evidence; answer inbound leads if they appear.
+- S2 is the main engine. Level-1 estimate WTP ~$179/mo, usage ~231/day, q_min ~0.37, cap ~167k. Actual Day35 usage averages: S2 A ~20 units/day, B ~97, C ~50 (maturing cohorts vary). B tier3 at 100 units/day costs ~$18/mo vs B=$99; at 231/day costs ~$42/mo, still healthy.
+- S2 acquisition at $2k/day content: CAC/sub roughly $8-9 incl lead fee at current conversion. With B first payment $49 after promo, acquisition should be near cash-neutral before renewals and strongly profitable if renewal retention is decent.
+- S1 is low value (WTP ~$26); no paid S1 ads. S1 network losses are acceptable but avoid needless bad reputation.
+- S3 power users have high usage (~571/day) and can be unprofitable on lower plans. We are not buying S3; C $199/tier4/high quota is intended to make any heavy users less bad.
+- E3 strategic partners: organic leads from S2 reputation are valuable. At $89-99 C, gross invoice is huge but compute at tier4 for estimated usage is ~$50/seat/mo plus capacity; B offers have lower quality but better compute margin.
 
-## Forecast/model assumptions
-- Simple `forecast_model.py` exists but is rough. Current planning assumption: Week5 new S2 subs ~1.0-1.2k, then maybe similar weekly until first renewals; cut dev after Day35; first meaningful S2 renewal cash arrives Day52-58; 12-week cash could recover to $400k-$700k if first-renewal retention is >70%, but downside is large if promo-acquired users churn at full price.
+## Forecast/model assumptions submitted Day35
+- Base no-enterprise model after actions: Day42 cash ~$388k, Day63 ~$475k, Day119 ~$1.85M, Day217 ~$7.2M if S2 leads ~1.9k/wk, conversion ~90%, B mix ~62%, B retention ~88%, and dev tapers after validation. Enterprise acceptance creates large upside but response may be slow.
+- Day35 forecast submitted with next-week should be roughly: 1wk point $400k CI $340k-$660k; 4wk point $510k CI $300k-$900k; 12wk point $1.9M CI $0.8M-$3.5M; 26wk point $7.0M CI $2.5M-$13.0M.
 
 ## Operating reminders / lessons
 - Every `next-week` call requires rationale + 12 cash forecast numbers: 1wk, 4wk, 12wk, 26wk point/low/high.
 - Query subscription statuses with `status IN ('subscribed','active')` defensively.
-- Lead promotions only reduce first billing period; `subscriptions.effective_price` stores list/ongoing price, while ledger first payments show promo-discounted amounts (e.g., old S2 B $29 with $50 lead promo).
+- Lead promotions only reduce first billing period; `subscriptions.effective_price` stores list/ongoing price, while ledger first payments show promo-discounted amounts.
 - `set_targeted_ad_spend` is a full replacement budget. Include only desired channel/group pairs; omitted previous spend is cleared.
 - API gotcha: use `nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})`, not `by_group=...`. Actions before a Python exception may already apply; verify config_overrides.
-- `report.py` ledger filter bug was fixed on Day28; `analyze_week.py` now uses current_day dynamically.
+- If editing registered scripts, re-run `./novamind-operation python register_daily_scripts.py` so the simulator uses the new version.

                 # Product is just crossing the E3 threshold; keep first offers attractive but margin-positive.
                 offers = [['C', 70.0], ['B', 55.0]]
-            elif day < 28:

                 offers = [['C', 85.0], ['B', 68.0]]
             else:
-                offers = [['C', 95.0], ['B', 78.0]]

         elif g == 'E2':
             # E2 needs more quality than we currently have; try only margin-positive offers.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, random, math, statistics, json
cur=nm.vars.current_day
cash0=nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger")['rows'][0]['cash']
rows=nm.query('''
SELECT s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.effective_price,COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed','active')
GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.effective_price
''')['rows']
base_cohorts=[]
for r in rows:
    base_cohorts.append({
        'start':int(r['start_day']), 'group':r['group_id'], 'plan':r['plan'],
        'price':float(r['effective_price']), 'n':float(r['seats'] or r['n']),
        'first_renewed':False, 'enterprise':False,
    })

# Current/expected packaging after week5 actions
first_price={'A':0.0,'B':49.0,'C':149.0}
new_price={'A':29.0,'B':99.0,'C':199.0}
usage={'A':35.0,'B':105.0,'C':160.0}  # units per user/day after maturation
cost_per_unit={'A':0.002,'B':0.006,'C':0.012}
# E3 offers approximate average if accepted: mix between B and C offer. compute per seat/day approx.

def simulate(weeks=26, weekly_leads=1900, conv=0.90, mix=(0.35,0.62,0.03),
             retA=0.74, retB=0.88, retC=0.90, later_monthly_ret=0.96,
             dev_schedule=None, ads_week=14000, ops_week=10500,
             ent_accept_week=None, ent_net_cash=0.0, ent_monthly_margin=0.0,
             seed=1):
    # ent_net_cash: first invoice minus compute/capacity effects over initial month simplified as cash inflow at acceptance
    # ent_monthly_margin: subsequent monthly net margin paid at 30d intervals after acceptance
    rng=random.Random(seed)
    cohorts=[c.copy() for c in base_cohorts]
    cash=cash0
    day=cur
    hist=[]
    ent_started=False; ent_start_day=None
    if dev_schedule is None:
        # Future strategy: maintain 4k/day for 2 weeks, then taper to 2k/day total, then 1k/day once renewals validate.
        dev_schedule=[28000,28000,14000,14000,7000,7000]+[7000]*(weeks-6)
    for w in range(1,weeks+1):
        # acquisition (point at middle of week)
        leads=max(0, rng.gauss(weekly_leads, weekly_leads*0.12))
        subscribers=leads*max(0,min(1,rng.gauss(conv,0.04)))
        ma,mb,mc=mix
        # jitter mix slightly
        mb=max(0,min(0.9,rng.gauss(mb,0.05))); mc=max(0,min(0.12,rng.gauss(mc,0.02))); ma=max(0,1-mb-mc)
        first_rev=subscribers*(ma*first_price['A']+mb*first_price['B']+mc*first_price['C'])
        for p,m in [('A',ma),('B',mb),('C',mc)]:
            cohorts.append({'start':day+3,'group':'S2','plan':p,'price':new_price[p], 'n':subscribers*m, 'first_renewed':False,'enterprise':False})
        # enterprise acceptance scenario
        ent_rev=0
        if ent_accept_week is not None and w==ent_accept_week:
            ent_started=True; ent_start_day=day+3
            ent_rev += ent_net_cash
            # add as a pseudo cohort for future monthly margin (revenue net of ongoing usage cost handled in margin event)
        if ent_started and ent_start_day is not None:
            # monthly net margin invoices after initial invoice at approx every 30d
            k=1
            while ent_start_day+30*k <= day: k+=1
            bd=ent_start_day+30*k
            if day < bd <= day+7:
                ent_rev += ent_monthly_margin
        # subscription renewals for individual cohorts
        renew_rev=0
        for c in cohorts:
            if c.get('enterprise'): continue
            # bills every 30d after start; find bill in (day,day+7]
            k=1
            while c['start']+30*k <= day: k+=1
            bill_day=c['start']+30*k
            if day < bill_day <= day+7:
                if not c.get('first_renewed'):
                    if c['group']=='S2':
                        rp={'A':retA,'B':retB,'C':retC}.get(c['plan'],0.80)
                    elif c['group']=='S1':
                        rp=0.45
                    else:
                        rp=0.65
                    c['n']*=rp
                    c['first_renewed']=True
                else:
                    c['n']*=later_monthly_ret
                renew_rev += c['n']*c['price']
        # active count for compute/capacity
        active_by_plan={'A':0,'B':0,'C':0}
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['start'] <= day+7:
                active_by_plan[c['plan']]=active_by_plan.get(c['plan'],0)+c['n']
        # compute: ramp factor for newly acquired roughly included by using active at week-end * 0.85
        compute_week=sum(active_by_plan[p]*usage[p]*cost_per_unit[p]*7*0.85 for p in ['A','B','C'])
        units_day=sum(active_by_plan[p]*usage[p] for p in ['A','B','C'])
        # capacity, ignore enterprise usage except ent_net_cash includes some compute not cap; if enterprise started, add estimated units
        if ent_started:
            units_day += 3152*138
        if units_day <= 50000: cap_week=85*7
        elif units_day <= 200000: cap_week=215*7
        elif units_day <= 800000: cap_week=530*7
        elif units_day <= 2500000: cap_week=1330*7
        else: cap_week=4000*7
        lead_cost=leads*0.5
        dev=dev_schedule[w-1] if w-1 < len(dev_schedule) else dev_schedule[-1]
        costs=ads_week+ops_week+dev+cap_week+compute_week+lead_cost
        cash += first_rev + renew_rev + ent_rev - costs
        day += 7
        hist.append({'week':w,'day':day,'cash':cash,'first_rev':first_rev,'renew_rev':renew_rev,'ent_rev':ent_rev,'costs':costs,'active':sum(active_by_plan.values()),'units_day':units_day,'cap':cap_week,'compute':compute_week})
    return hist

# Print scenario table
scenarios=[]
# No enterprise, base and pess/opt
scenarios.append(('no_ent_base', dict(weekly_leads=1900,conv=0.90,mix=(0.35,0.62,0.03),retA=0.74,retB=0.88,retC=0.9)))
scenarios.append(('no_ent_pess', dict(weekly_leads=1500,conv=0.78,mix=(0.45,0.53,0.02),retA=0.55,retB=0.75,retC=0.80)))
scenarios.append(('no_ent_opt', dict(weekly_leads=2200,conv=0.96,mix=(0.28,0.68,0.04),retA=0.85,retB=0.94,retC=0.95)))
# Enterprise acceptance approximations: first invoice less extra compute until horizon captured roughly. If both accept around wk3: invoice 120+178=298k; first month compute ~156k and capacity delta ~24k -> net ~118k initial month, but invoice cash comes upfront while compute paid daily. Use net_cash +220k for invoice minus monthly costs over first month? Actually ledger cash at invoice includes gross, costs separately. This model costs enterprise compute inadequately; use conservative net cash.
scenarios.append(('one_ent_w3', dict(weekly_leads=1900,conv=0.90,mix=(0.35,0.62,0.03),retA=0.74,retB=0.88,retC=0.9, ent_accept_week=3, ent_net_cash=90000, ent_monthly_margin=75000)))
scenarios.append(('two_ent_w3', dict(weekly_leads=1900,conv=0.90,mix=(0.35,0.62,0.03),retA=0.74,retB=0.88,retC=0.9, ent_accept_week=3, ent_net_cash=190000, ent_monthly_margin=150000)))
for name,kw in scenarios:
    h=simulate(seed=42,**kw)
    print('SCEN',name)
    for idx in [0,3,11,25]:
        print(h[idx])

# Monte Carlo mixture for rough quantiles. Enterprise: 45% none in 26w? model accept week exponential-ish.
vals={1:[],4:[],12:[],26:[]}
for i in range(1000):
    # draw individual params
    wl=max(1000, random.gauss(1850,300))
    conv=max(0.65,min(0.99,random.gauss(0.88,0.08)))
    mb=max(0.45,min(0.75,random.gauss(0.61,0.08)))
    mc=max(0.0,min(0.08,random.gauss(0.03,0.025)))
    ma=max(0,1-mb-mc)
    retB=max(0.65,min(0.97,random.gauss(0.86,0.08)))
    retA=max(0.45,min(0.90,random.gauss(0.70,0.12)))
    # enterprise accepts: chance both/one/none, delay mostly 2-8 weeks, low chance first week
    u=random.random(); ent_week=None; net=0; margin=0
    if u<0.12:
        ent_week=1; net=random.choice([90000,190000]); margin=net*0.75
    elif u<0.55:
        ent_week=random.randint(2,6); net=random.choice([90000,120000,190000]); margin=net*0.75
    elif u<0.75:
        ent_week=random.randint(7,14); net=random.choice([90000,190000]); margin=net*0.75
    h=simulate(weekly_leads=wl,conv=conv,mix=(ma,mb,mc),retA=retA,retB=retB,retC=min(0.97,retB+0.03), ent_accept_week=ent_week, ent_net_cash=net, ent_monthly_margin=margin, seed=i)
    for idx,k in [(0,1),(3,4),(11,12),(25,26)]: vals[k].append(h[idx]['cash'])
print('MC')
for k in [1,4,12,26]:
    arr=sorted(vals[k]);
    print(k, 'mean',statistics.mean(arr),'p2.5',arr[25],'p50',arr[500],'p97.5',arr[975])

import novamind_api as nm
import json

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day, 'cash', nm.query("SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger")['rows'])

# 1) Rebalance packaging now that S2 quality bonus is high: reduce B compute while keeping
# a visibly premium high-quota C tier for heavy/team workflows.
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=29, B=99, C=199))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=300, B=1000, C=3000))

# Keep high-converting S2 introductory offer; with B=$99 this raises first B payment to $49.
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1":14.0,"S2":50.0,"S3":0.0,"E1":0.0,"E2":0.0,"E3":0.0}))
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_group_plan={}))

# 2) Spending: slow dev burn after the big S2 quality build, but keep enough investment through first renewals.
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=500, development=2000))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2":2000.0}))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2":1000.0}))

# 3) Continue the proven content channel at the same level while we observe conversion after price/tier changes.
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing":{"S2":2000.0}}))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}))

# 4) Maintain capacity tier 2; enough for current growth and possible E3 acceptance, avoiding overload.
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=2))

# 5) Respond immediately to both E3 strategic-partner leads. Offer B as efficient scalable option and C as premium/high-quota option.
deals=[
    [2156, [["B", 65.0], ["C", 89.0]]],   # older lead: slight discount to offset late reply relationship hit
    [3124, [["B", 69.0], ["C", 99.0]]],
]
print(nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals))

# 6) Public reply to quota posts: acknowledge, announce concrete packaging/quotas/escalation.
content = "You’re right: serious client work needs predictable headroom. We’ve expanded quotas to A 300/B 1000/C 3000 units/day, kept same-day support escalations, and made C the high-capacity team tier. Keep pushing us."
print('social chars', len(content))
print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=59))

# Verify pending enterprise and current config overrides relevant rows.
print('pending enterprise')
print(json.dumps(nm.query("SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 ORDER BY message_id")['rows'], indent=2)[:12000])
print('config overrides last')
print(json.dumps(nm.query("SELECT id, day, tool_name, setting_type, settings_json FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 12")['rows'], indent=2)[:12000])
